title: Work from home and drive more slowly to save energy, global body urges
summary:
• The International Energy Agency is urging people to work from home and drive more slowly to save energy amidst global market concerns.
• Damage to energy infrastructure in Gulf countries is expected to reduce oil and gas exports, exacerbating global energy market problems.
• The suggested lifestyle changes are intended to mitigate the effects of reduced energy supplies and help stabilize the global energy market.

title: UK borrowing costs hit highest level since 2008 financial crisis
summary:
• The UK's borrowing costs have reached their highest level since the 2008 financial crisis, indicating a significant increase in the cost of government borrowing.
• According to the Office for National Statistics (ONS), the UK's government debt is estimated to be around 93.1% of the size of the UK economy as of February 2026.
• This level of government debt is comparable to levels last seen in the early 1960s, highlighting the substantial burden of debt on the UK economy.

title: Typical energy bill forecast to rise by £332 a year in July
summary:
• The typical energy bill is forecast to rise by £332 a year in July, adding to the financial burden on households.
• There is an ongoing debate about whether support to mitigate this increase should be universally applied to all households or targeted at specific groups.
• A targeted approach, focusing on vulnerable and low-income households, would allow for more substantial assistance to those in need while also being more cost-effective for the government.

title: Trump-backed television merger moves forward
summary:
• A television merger backed by Trump is moving forward with the combined company set to own 15% of the country's television stations after the takeover.
• The company argues that keeping the current ownership limits in place would be counterproductive to the purpose of the agency regulations.
• The merger's progression suggests a shift in the regulatory approach to television station ownership, potentially paving the way for further consolidation in the industry.

title: Hargreaves Lansdown says IT issues which affected thousands are over
summary:
• Hargreaves Lansdown has announced that the IT issues affecting its services are now resolved.
• The technical problems had impacted thousands of clients, causing disruptions to their trading and account access.
• Some clients, such as Gerardo Vece, had investments that were particularly vulnerable to the outage due to their volatile nature and short-term trading strategy.

title: EU companies pursue sustainability reporting despite CSRD rollbacks: report
summary:
• Ninety percent of European companies that are no longer required to report under the EU's Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive still plan to maintain or expand their sustainability reporting activity.
• Almost 90% of companies surveyed expect to increase their investment in sustainability reporting and automation over the coming year, despite expecting reduced regulatory scrutiny.
• The majority of companies have already integrated sustainability reporting with financial reporting processes, with 90% of respondents indicating that this integration is either partial or full, and expecting it to deepen in the coming year.

title: FL retiree lost her $135K nest egg to a scam call — and crimes targeting seniors are surging. How to protect yourself
summary:
• A Florida retiree, Carol Ann Moritz, lost her entire $135,000 nest egg to scammers posing as bank officials and federal agents, who convinced her to wire the money as part of a supposed investigation.
• The scam involved multiple people, with the scammers using official-sounding language and sending fake messages to make the situation appear legitimate, which is a common tactic used by sophisticated scammers to gain the victim's trust.
• Retired FBI special agent Keith Givens warns that these types of scams are surging, and understanding how they work can help people, especially seniors, avoid becoming victims by being cautious of phone calls and requests for money transfers.

title: Investors Are Piling Into This 'Boring' Asset For Gains
summary:
• Investors are increasingly putting their money into bond ETFs, which have been outperforming the S&P 500 this year.
• A significant $14.1 billion was invested in U.S. bond ETFs during the week ended March 13, according to ETF.com.
• This large investment in bond ETFs accounts for more than half of the total $27.3 billion that flowed into all ETFs during the same week.

title: Lumentum Climbs On Optical Networking Technology Outlook, Nvidia GTC News
summary:
• Lumentum's stock price has increased due to a positive outlook on its optical networking technology.
• Nvidia's GTC news has also contributed to the positive sentiment in the technology sector, particularly for companies like Lumentum.
• The overall market, however, is experiencing a downturn, with the major indexes breaking long-term support due to rising oil prices and bond yields.

title: UK regulator probes Photoshop maker Adobe over cancellation fee concerns
summary:
• The UK's Competition and Markets Authority has launched an investigation into Adobe to examine whether its early cancellation fees for products like Photoshop are unfair and misleading to customers.
• The regulator will look at whether customers are given clear and timely information upfront about these fees, which could influence their decision to purchase Adobe's products, including Photoshop, Illustrator, and Adobe Premiere.
• The probe is part of the CMA's use of its new direct consumer enforcement powers, which allow it to determine whether consumer law has been breached and take action, including securing consumer redress and imposing fines on companies where wrongdoing is found.

title: History says these 2 overlooked asset classes are the only real shield against 1970s-style stagflation
summary:
• Historical data suggests that small-cap stocks and housing are two often-overlooked asset classes that can provide protection against stagflation.
• Unlike other assets, small-caps and housing have been shown to hold their value during periods of high inflation and stagnant economic growth, such as the 1970s.
• As a result, investors seeking a shield against potential stagflation may want to consider allocating a portion of their portfolios to small-cap stocks and housing, rather than relying solely on traditional assets.

title: Here’s the exact amount of money you need to make to be financially secure
summary:
• The yearly threshold for achieving financial security for one-person households over age 65 is roughly $62,700.
• For families with two or more adults and three or more kids, the yearly threshold for achieving financial security is approximately $169,300.
• The thresholds for achieving financial security vary significantly based on the size and composition of the household, as well as the age of the individuals.

title: Global oil prices climb for 5th straight week as U.S. sends more military might into Middle East
summary:
• Global oil prices have increased for the fifth consecutive week, with Brent crude leading the rise.
• The surge in oil prices is attributed to the U.S. deploying more military troops and warships to the Middle East, heightening tensions in the region.
• The Pentagon's decision to increase its military presence in the Middle East has contributed to the ongoing upward trend in global oil prices, with Brent crude settling higher on Friday.

title: March 27 looks like a make-or-break day for American travelers and the partial government shutdown
summary:
• The partial government shutdown is causing increasing pain for American travelers, particularly in terms of longer TSA wait times at airports.
• March 27 is being seen as a critical day that could either bring an end to the shutdown or exacerbate the problems faced by travelers and federal workers.
• On March 27, thousands of federal workers, including TSA agents and air traffic controllers, will miss their second paycheck, potentially leading to increased absences and further disruptions to air travel.

title: Gold isn’t your safe haven in this war: It just logged its biggest weekly drop in over 14 years
summary:
• Gold prices experienced their largest weekly percentage drop in over 14 years, marking a significant decline.
• This drop is unusual because gold is typically considered a safe-haven asset that increases in value during times of conflict and uncertainty.
• The decline in gold prices occurred despite the ongoing escalation of the conflict in Iran, which would normally be expected to drive up demand for safe-haven assets like gold.

title: Chuck Norris Quietly Built a Surprising Business Empire
summary:
• Chuck Norris has built a diverse business empire that includes various ventures such as karate studios and bottled water.
• The business empire was established by Norris long before he retired from his acting career, showcasing his entrepreneurial spirit.
• The range of businesses owned by Chuck Norris is surprising, given his reputation as a famous actor, and demonstrates his ability to succeed in multiple industries.

title: Micron’s CEO Warns: AI Laptop Prices Are About to Soar—and It Won’t End Soon
summary:
• Micron's CEO has issued a warning that the prices of AI laptops are about to increase significantly.
• The main factors driving this price surge are surging demand, constrained supply, and rising memory requirements for AI laptops.
• According to the warning, the price increase is not expected to be a short-term issue, but rather a prolonged trend in the market for AI laptops.

title: Google and Apple Never Hire ‘UX/UI Designers.’ Neither Should You
summary:
• Google and Apple do not hire employees with the specific job title of 'UX/UI Designers', indicating a potential shift in industry hiring practices.
• The decision to not hire 'UX/UI Designers' may be due to the broad and often misleading nature of the job title, which can encompass a wide range of skills and responsibilities.
• Companies may benefit from adopting a more nuanced approach to hiring designers, focusing on specific skill sets and specialties rather than relying on the general 'UX/UI Designer' title.

title: Would You Get a Drink in the Kendra Scott Cocktail Bar?
summary:
• The Kendra Scott jewelry brand is introducing a new brick-and-mortar experience in the form of a Western stable-themed bar.
• This unique bar is located in Nashville, offering a distinctive setting for customers to socialize and enjoy drinks.
• By opening a cocktail bar, Kendra Scott is venturing into a new kind of retail experience that combines entertainment with its brand presence.

title: Industry Experts Just Named the Best Bourbon in a Blind Tasting—And It’s Under $70
summary:
• The Beverage Testing Institute conducted a blind tasting of thousands of American whiskeys to determine the best bourbon.
• Industry experts participated in the tasting to evaluate the different bourbon options and identify the top choice.
• The best bourbon selected by the experts is priced under $70, making it an affordable option for those looking to try a high-quality bourbon.
